articles.title: Chatbots quietly agree with everything you say, and researchers reveal how simple prompt changes can fix the problem
articles.description: Users unknowingly influence AI answers through framing and tone choices
articles.content:
Chatbots often mirror user opinions instead of challenging assumptions directly
Confident wording significantly increases agreement levels in large language models
Question-based prompts reduce sycophantic responses across tested AI systems

articles.title: Canada’s AI minister says Anthropic withholding Mythos is ‘responsible’
articles.description: Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon met on Tuesday with representatives of Anthropic — the company that said its latest chatbot, Mythos, is too risky for public release.
articles.content:
Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon met on Tuesday with representatives of Anthropic — the company that said its latest chatbot, Mythos, is too risky for public release last week.

articles.title: What is Trump's approval rating amid AI-Jesus, pope controversy
articles.description: President Donald Trump has had a historically low approval rating for about a year. Now, he is going after Pope Leo. What do people think of the pope?
articles.content:
President Trump criticized Pope Leo XIV on Truth Social after the pope questioned his immigration tactics and war in Iran.
Trump's approval rating is net negative, with various polls showing his disapproval rating around 57%.

articles.description: Comedian Jon Stewart took aim at Donald Trump after a controversial AI-generated image depicting the former US president as Jesus went viral, sparking outrage and ridicule online.

articles.title: Global Health Innovations and Regulatory Shifts
articles.description: Recent health news highlights significant regulatory and technological advancements in the medical field. South Korea bans syringe stockpiling due to supply concerns amid the Iran conflict, while U.S. companies like Amazon and Novo Nordisk leverage AI in drug development. The U.S. FDA grants approvals, expands trials, and investigates potential chemical risks in consumer products.
articles.content:
South Korea is banning the stockpiling of medical needles and syringes to prevent supply disruptions, especially during ongoing geopolitical tensions impacting global supply chains, the finance ministry announced.
In the United States, the FDA has ap... [562 chars]

articles.title: Over 70% of Japan high school students use AI, mostly as study assistant: survey
articles.description: A total of 73.7 percent of Japanese high school students are using conversational artificial intelligence, with the most common purpose being to serve as study assistant and collect information, a survey showed. According to the Gakken Research Institute for Learning and Education survey, 36.6 percent of elementary school students and…
articles.content:
